reviews.author: Joanne Koong (author)
reviews.summary: The author addressed a concern about missing forget requests in error paths, explaining that these paths only trigger with misbehaving servers returning invalid attributes and that the real-world impact is minimal. The author agreed to send a forget request to tell the server to release its reference.

reviews.author: Joanne Koong (author)
reviews.summary: The author addressed the concern about using goto statements in fuse_lookup_name() by removing them and directly returning instead. The code was also simplified by assigning fm->fc to a local variable, fc, and using that throughout the function.

reviews.author: Joanne Koong (author)
reviews.summary: The author addressed the concern about gotos with direct returns in fuse_lookup() by removing them and simplifying the d_splice_alias error path, confirming that no functional changes were intended.

reviews.author: Joanne Koong (author)
reviews.summary: Author addressed Amir's concern about the code structure in dir.c, specifically fuse_dentry_revalidate(), by removing gotos and flattening nested if/else structures, confirming no functional changes are intended.

reviews.author: Joanne Koong (author)
reviews.summary: The author is addressing a concern about the use of goto statements in fuse_create_open() and has removed it, replacing it with a direct return statement.

reviews.author: Joanne Koong (author)
reviews.summary: The author addressed a concern about the code structure in dir.c by moving flag validation into the flags branch and returning early to eliminate the else block, making no functional changes.

reviews.author: Joanne Koong (author)
reviews.summary: Author addressed a concern about unnecessary code in fuse_time_to_jiffies() by removing an else statement, confirming no functional changes were intended.

reviews.author: Joanne Koong (author)
reviews.summary: Author addressed a concern about the use of goto in fuse_link() and agreed to restructure it by removing the goto statement and replacing it with an else if for -ENOSYS check, confirming no functional changes.

reviews.author: Joanne Koong (author)
reviews.summary: Author addressed a concern about if-else nesting in the code structure of fuse_dentry_revalidate(), agreed to restructure logic to reduce nesting, and provided an updated patch that includes these changes.

2026-04-23.patch_summary: This patchset is a series of cleanups in the fuse: dir.c file, addressing code structure issues pointed out by Amir. The changes aim to improve the maintainability and readability of the code without introducing any functional changes, except for the first patch which fixes missing forget requests in error paths.

reviews.author: Amir Goldstein
reviews.summary: Reviewer argued that the cleanup patches are not worth the effort due to potential backporting conflicts and excessive churn, requesting to drop all but the fuse_dentry_revalidate() patch.
reviews.raw_body:
I think this is not a good idea.
These sort of cleanup (e.g. removing unneeded gotos) are a kin to
whitespace cleanups - we should not do them unless going to change
the function for another reason.

The churn this creates and backporting conflicts are not worth the
win of the cleanup itself.

Therefore, please drop all but the fuse_dentry_revalidate() patch
which should be part of your passthrough series.

I will review only this one patch.

Thanks,
Amir.

reviews.author: Amir Goldstein
reviews.summary: reviewer noticed a repeated pattern in fuse_dentry_revalidate() and suggested hiding it behind a helper function that validates the expected attributes
reviews.raw_body:
This seems like a repeating pattern.
Would you consider hiding this behind a helper that validates we got
an expected set of attr:

        if (fuse_invalid_attr_ftype(&outarg.attr, mode & S_IFMT))

Thanks,
Amir.

reviews.author: Amir Goldstein
reviews.summary:
Reviewer questioned the usefulness of a local variable, suggesting it's only used once and its name doesn't provide clear information

Reviewer suggested that a kfree should be done before the nlookup++ block, noting that the current line spacing in the code is also weird

Reviewer noted that fuse_dentry_revalidate() has a code structure issue, but did not specify the exact problem or request any changes.
reviews.raw_body:
I am not sure that this help var is really useful?
It is used just once in the next line and its name does not clarify
something that needs to be clarified.

---

Nit: line spacing is weird.
I would do this kfree before the nlookup++ block.

---

Apart from the nits, feel free to add:
Reviewed-by: Amir Goldstein <amir73il@gmail.com>

Thanks,
Amir.

reviews.author: Joanne Koong (author)
reviews.summary: Author acknowledged a potential issue with backporting conflicts, decided to drop the current patchset and include only the fuse_dentry_revalidate() cleanup in a future passthrough series.
reviews.raw_body:
Ah, I hadn't considered the backporting conflicts. That makes sense  -
I'll keep that in mind for the future.

I'll drop this series and include only the fuse_dentry_revalidate()
cleanup and have that as part of the passthrough series.

Thanks,
Joanne

reviews.author: Joanne Koong (author)
reviews.summary: Author agreed to address a specific concern about code structure in fuse_dentry_revalidate() by adding a new helper function.
reviews.raw_body:
I will add this helper.

Thanks,
Joanne

2026-04-24.patch_summary: This patchset is a series of cleanups in the fuse: dir.c file, addressing code structure issues pointed out by Amir. The changes aim to improve the maintainability and readability of the code without introducing any functional changes, except for the first patch which fixes missing forget requests in error paths.
